title: cerastium brachypetalum subsp. tauricum
intro: Cerastium brachypetalum subsp. tauricum, also known as the short-petaled mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of the Cerastium brachypetalum plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cerastium brachypetalum subsp. tauricum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns, as well as its potential to support immune system function and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by infusing its leaves in hot water or combining it with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and topical creams for external use.
